'''Brad Davis''' (born 30 April 1982) is a [[rugby league]] footballer who previously played for the [[Gold Coast Titans]] in the [[National Rugby League]]. He played as a [[Rugby league positions#Scrum-half|half-back]].

==Early life==
Davis grew up in Werris Creek NSW. He participated in many Catholic School Representative teams and other schoolboy football teams.

==Playing career==
In 2007 Davis helped the [[Tweed Heads Seagulls]] become the first NSW based team to win the [[Queensland Cup]], winning the inaugural Duncal Hall Medal for man-of-the-match in the grand final.<ref>{{cite news
|last=Ross 
|first=Livermore 
|title=Australian Rugby Football League Annual Report 2007 
|pages=9 
|publisher=Australian Rugby League Limited 
|year=2007 
|url=http://www.australianrugbyleague.com.au/files/ARL_2007_Annrep_1.pdf 
|access-date=2009-07-15 
|url-status=dead 
|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090913145959/http://www.australianrugbyleague.com.au/files/ARL_2007_Annrep_1.pdf 
|archive-date=13 September 2009 
|df=dmy 
}}</ref>

Davis made his [[National Rugby League]] debut in 2008, playing in six matches for the [[Gold Coast Titans]].<ref name=rlp/>

Davis is the [[Tweed Heads Seagulls]] leading points scorer of all time. He scored a total of 677 points in 92 games for the club in the [[Queensland Cup]].<ref name="Tweed Heads Seagulls">{{cite web|url=http://www.qrl.com.au/default.aspx?s=qldcup-clubs-tweed|title=Tweed Heads Seagulls|publisher=QRL|year=2010|access-date=2010-03-15|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110218232205/http://www.qrl.com.au/default.aspx?s=qldcup-clubs-tweed|archive-date=18 February 2011|url-status=dead|df=dmy-all}}</ref>

==Post career==
Davis coaches a Palm Beach Currumbin Sports High rugby league team. He is currently the Transition Coach for the Gold Coast Titans.
